Name: 0_data_data science_science_data scientist
Representative_Docs: ['data business analyst would still be a hard sell the type of analytical work they do is pretty far from what a data scientist would do at least i haven t seen a business data analyst move diagonally into a data scientist role without some formal phd master degree the problem is business data analyst is typically banded lower than a data scientist engineer so they re still looking for the master phd background if you just want to work with data then data business analyst maybe is not a terrible end goal being a data scientist engineer on the other hand is a whole other ballgame i m not saying theres no overlap but sample the job descriptions from both paths and you would probably start sensing the difference long story short it s a terribly steep uphill battle to do data science related stuff without masters phd arguable still kinda hard with just a masters given the amount of phds applying for those roles unless you trying to be a product manager or software engineer on a data science related team where you re not directly actually touching the data science models experiments analysis', 'depends entirely on your specialization data science is not an exclusive subset of computer science there will probably be less people working on developing models as models become more standardized resulting in less people being able to do largely the same but the amount of people working on analysis human work until strong ai becomes a thing and specific technical implementation is only going to increase not to mention as the field develops new job opportunities arise as have already development and processing of knowledge information is at the heart of a first world economy and data science simply seeks to streamline and automate that process similar to how computer science in it s inception automated logic and arithmetic every stat math physics and operations research phd wants to be a data scientist when they can t find a job in their field this is also completely not true quite a few stat majors and some computer science majors have an interest in data science but hardly all most computer science students still choose to go into theoretics phd software engineering networking and management specializations like security data science is a new niche that is still developing and is not as popular as the standard routes which are most safe and secure in job prospect generally speaking computer science engineering is not a difficult major in regards to the sciences at least not as difficult as mathematics chemistry or physics and the mathematical background required for master phd level ml and ds is quite a bit tougher than the other options available to computer scientists tensorflow api will become easier to use for software engineers and data analysts which will improve the productivity in data science leading to potentially more jobs it s a good thing except for perhaps those who are specialized in developing models but those can just as easily use their background to cover analysis or implementation companies still require people with heavyweight knowledge in statistics and proper model interpretation data scientists will be paid less even now sometimes than software engineers completely different jobs and completely untrue most people in the field of data are veteran software engineers there are little if none entry level data jobs but plenty of entry level software jobs only small start ups perhaps will hire data specialized people without experience but obviously the salary will suffer the natural course is a background in computer science experience in development and grow towards data fields through experience in automation and or statistics in combination with the low level technical details', 'data scientist come from types of backgrounds but majority come from either a computer science hard math statistics physics etc background it also depends on exactly what part of data science you want to work within data science there are data engineers data scientist ui engineers ai machine learning engineers etc it is supposedly going to increase in demand over the next years but who knows in general as a data scientist you should be able to work as a data scientist in different verticals industries as long as it s a general data scientist role']

Name: 1_python_language_languages_java
Representative_Docs: ['edit i am changing the last category to be named graduate graduate this one in particular was generating some controversy and i felt it was detracting from the post this post wasn t about looking at people who have been in the industry for several years to a decade and more about reflecting on what kinds of things cs students have the opportunity to be exposed to edit i was inspired to change advanced to junior by someone in the comments i feel this is an even better name hi everyone before i start i want to say that this is not a definitive guide the hope is that this will be useful to self taught hobbyist people switching careers who are wondering some of the things they should know to stay competitive with computer science graduates it will also be useful for anyone who is trying to get ahead of their existing coursework or is concerned that their skills are not where they should be as for where this post is coming from it is a combination of what i personally saw and was taught in school what i taught myself outside of school and what i saw on the job common university programming languages these are languages that are often taught in universities you don t need to know all of them the point is that i see a lot of people asking what language should i learn and the answer is that there is no one language that modern programming courses focus on in fact universities often choose languages that are best suited to illustrate the particular concepts that the course wants to cover java c these are often chosen to introduce people to things like object oriented programming oop c c used to teach things like the fundamentals of memory management since neither have garbage collectors also heavily used in computer architecture computer operating systems courses assembly often confined to things like computer architecture for examining how a c program gets translated to a low level form before machine code python this one is very popular for early introductory courses as well as more advanced courses like ai and machine learning scheme haskell these are often used to introduce people to functional declarative programming styles as well as to cement concepts related to recursion inside of people s minds javascript usually confined to classes related to web development but i ve also seen it used to introduce people to programming since it can run in everyone s browsers matlab this one is very popular once you get into math centric courses like numerical computing so as you can see if you re learning a language on the side chances are someone is learning some of the same stuff in a college class how language use evolves in college in this section i want to explain how the use of language changes as people go through their college courses you might find your own journey mirroring this even if you aren t taking the traditional route lower level undergrad a lot of effort is put into deciding the best language to use to introduce people to programming common choices are java and python the teacher will tell you ahead of time what you will be using and they will spend a lot of time introducing basic concepts upper level undergrad much less time is spent on introducing the basics and some classes will even attempt to teach more than one programming language if it fits i e scheme and haskell c and assembly close to bachelor s into master s often there is little to no introduction the professor will either say here is a handout for the language we use and expect you to pick up the basics on your own or they will say there is no set language for this class choose the one you think is best they essentially expect that you are good enough to pick up languages quickly and are experienced enough to select one that is well suited for the coursework the point is this don t get attached to any one language learning how to pick up new things efficiently should be part of the journey now i will begin going through the different programmer levels these levels should help to understand what you should strive for as you feel yourself becoming more and more skilled don t worry much about what level this list places you at just focus on where you re going beginner has little to no programming experience this means that they are just getting exposed to the kind of mindset you have to adopt to program effectively and it is likely a difficult initial step either has not or just recently chose a programming language they want to learn since there are so many new things they need to learn it is usually best that they stick with a single language for a while until they feel very comfortable branching out control flow is still a very strange concept consistent use of if statements switch cases and loops often takes up a lot of initial practice time breaking up code into logical units i e functions is done sparingly as much code as possible is placed into startup functions likely spends a lot of time in getting started types of tutorials in order to begin forming their foundation in their language of choice often finds themselves feeling overwhelmed and confused since everything is so new largest program they have written roughly in the ballpark of lines organized into one file intermediate understands the value of breaking up code into logical units suitable for reuse has been exposed to object oriented programming and is beginning to understand how to organize code into classes though this may still be a difficult area for them has been exposed to memory management in a language without a garbage collector understands the difference between the stack heap and why the distinction is important understands what recursion is even if they still find it very hard to write read code that uses it has been exposed to some of the foundations of the logic they have been using in programming such as boolean algebra most work is done through guis ide for writing code github gui if they have branched into that realm yet most all of their work is stored on their local hard drive has begun to explore the world of data structures and algorithms even if they find it incredibly confusing understands that teaching others what you learn is one of the best ways to reinforce what they already know they make use of this whenever possible exposed to the concepts of automating the build process using make cmake etc knows the difference between an interpreter and a compiler even if they haven t implemented either one largest program they have written probably ranges from lines which was either in one file or junior heavy reliance on command line tools such as git for version control developing this skill came from having to interface with multiple computers to deploy their programs i e linux shell via ssh much more developed use of build automation especially for their larger projects has moved away from storing everything on their local hard drive meaning shared drives are the norm for them if their main computer dies they are not concerned about much else except reinstalling their ides tool chains compilers has been exposed to the functional declarative style of programming with this exposure has come a new understanding of just how much influence they are beginning to exert on modern languages like java and c there is a firm understanding of iterative vs recursive styles and they are not afraid to switch between the two based on the nature of the problem at hand able to use at least one language very well and has toyed with at least others in order to broaden their horizons they see the connections between these other languages and the one they re best at but understands that they are distinctly unique at the end of the day understands at least coarsely how to reason about levels of complexity o n o log n o nlog n o n o n etc strong understanding of the pros cons of common data structures linked list array fixed size and dynamic trees hash tables queues and heaps with this understanding comes the ability to use the right data structure for the job knowledge of common algorithms and why they exist search binary search string search brute force kmp rabin karp graph search breadth first depth first best first shortest path dijkstra s a sorting bubble sort merge sort quicksort has delved into the realm of large software and tried their hand at it this was likely a difficult process since they have probably only designed or programs that required better organization tried multithreading and likely got upset but they have at least enough experience with it to be wary of it largest program they have written several thousand lines split up over a multitude of files each file corresponded to a specific system within their program though some were probably a little bloated due to being new to writing large programs graduate graduate graduate indicating master s students has been exposed to a variety of languages and has developed strategies for learning new ones quickly and efficiently not only have they been exposed to a variety of languages but they feel comfortable switching between them if one is more suited for a project than the other while they are still probably best at or languages they are at least skilled in a few others understands the importance of writing comprehensive test code this is one of the few ways to know whether new changes broke existing functionality all software they write makes use of build automation in order to make it easier to get the program ready to run especially if it needs to be deployed on a variety of platforms has been exposed to more formal software design principles after being burned a couple of times with their early large programs they now see the value of sitting down and hashing some of the core details out before they write a single line of code it is not uncommon for them to interface with a variety of computers running on different hardware and with different operating systems cross platform functionality has thus taken root in their minds and anytime they get to use a library that provides this for them they are beyond thankful speaking of libraries the graduate no longer finds the need to implement everything themselves whenever they are faced with a challenging problem they will first do some research to scope out existing libraries frameworks if they find one that is particularly suited for them they will not hesitate to use it they understand the importance of carefully designed interfaces especially when using libraries for example it is not uncommon for them to write special interface code that abstracts away their library choices in the event that they have to replace that library with something else not only do they have a strong understanding of common data structures but they have likely experimented with implementing more advanced ones like red black trees or hash maps or some of the concurrent data structures this brought a new respect for professionally implemented data structures and showed them some common pitfalls you can run into while trying to create a bullet proof implementation on your own still likes the idea of one man army style projects but sees the need to build strong communication and other team based skills large software takes a lot of talent more than any one person can hope to encompass largest program they have written lines of code split up over a multitude of files each file had a distinct purpose and did not deviate from it or deviated very little the interactions between software components were well thought out so as to keep things properly decoupled and avoid spaghetti monsters all code follows a consistent clean coding standard where computer science bachelor s leaves people programming wise only i would argue it s very possible that for someone who graduated with a bachelor s in computer science their programming skills will be between junior and graduate graduate there are also a lot of cases where people graduate with more of an intermediate junior skill set it just depends on the person how much effort they put in coursework internships personal projects for most people getting to graduate graduate and beyond with their programming skills won t happen until they get some real experience working for companies in a team environment for a few years anything beyond that will take much more time and a specialist skill set the workforce is just a different beast and really forces you to ramp up your skills to new heights in order to keep up', 'gt for example why would you chose c over java other than the optimisation here for a task main reason that came to my mind is performance there s a reason why c is a go to language for building video games engines for instance or why entire gpu computing relies it that second reason is that just about any other programming language was build in c or c this means that it s possible to create a high performance library using c and then import it to a higher level language that way you don t need to use c everywhere and just focus on vital parts in it writing rest of your code in something saner like python if rust was more popular and mature i would prefer to use it instead as it offers all power of c with far friendlier and safer syntax but alas it s still years before it can reach this stage for general public gt would you for a particular task first choose a language and then the tools for it language in itself is just a tool in my professional work i have used a bit of c c rust ruby and python i can also use r which is basically a free version of matlab to some degree if i needed to do something statistics related depends on what you are currently doing really and what are your specific expectations each language has their own use case after all eg if you want to do a gui application then natural choice is c or java c is superior for windows since it works with a single exe there java on the other hand works on all major platforms if you are doing automation and scripting python or ruby are phenomenal they might be slow languages to run but in exchange what you need to write lines in c you can do in one in ruby python also has very mature frameworks when it comes to everything scientific it s one of the most popular languages in this sector c or rust are best when you are building code for a specific platform as they are compiled languages they don t require any intermediate tools to work this isn t that much of a deal for a desktop but if you work with arduino that has kb of usable memory then you kinda can t fit anything different than compiled code there s no space for java environment plus they also let you manipulate memory directly which is often essential when it comes to performance and on it goes picking up a new programming language becomes simpler and simpler over time i have built fairly complex c applications with nothing but it s official documentation and a single book for instance gt do you have a particular pattern when looking for something would you eg try to find a framework or a library to do the thing you want to do you know i am not sure if i have a specific written thought process i would say that after enough time in programming you start having a tree of connections between different problems and possible tools you can use in your case i understood the problem as i have a code in matlab that does something with images it s slow so first i would actually look at said matlab toolbox implementation might be that it s actually really well optimized so i would stick with it i would also like to know what exactly is slow so some profiling is in order which part of your code actually takes most time one way or another there s no way in hell i could write a heavily optimized math related library not happening that would require actual mathematicians to help out and people with way more experience than me in low level programming so regardless of what tool i would choose next i would grab something that has history of being used in science and in high performance algorithms so i would be looking at c and eigen or maybe python and scipy numpy packages latter idea is actually more appealing at this point especially since opencv is easiest to use there but then you mention guis aka displaying said chart on a screen now this is annoying if i am doing this just for myself python would still work but if this was to deployed elsewhere it s kinda hard to make end user install a whole python environment seeing a mb file to display a single chart doesn t appeal to me either so now questions that pop to my head are windows only linux only both windows only c is really nice java is acceptable c might work as well linux only java maybe or c both c still fits if i use qt c is ruled out doesn t play with linux properly java remains a possibility too now image recognition is not a field i am particularly experienced at i have written optical character recognition code before and it somewhat worked with like accuracy but i know this field is currently under heavy research and is a very common task for neural networks and machine learning so even with opencv to help i would likely still require some studies so i would look at science papers sample code and books surrounding that subject if it was a work project i would skip science papers and just read a book or some blogs to get going faster with a prototype and then possibly improve it over time so i would say that there are no real shortcuts it s all about finding problems and trying to solve them on the way there you will learn a lot of new concepts and possibly find better solutions than what you originally envisioned later on', 'c is a well known computer programming language it s an object oriented language that pairs great with other languages such as python or java you can use c to develop pretty much anything it is used to write most application code in today s industry c was originally created by microsoft to compete with java java postings are more popular than any other language this is because a lot of companies run their back end systems with the language java is extremely popular among enterprise software according to a developer survey by stackoverflow java ranked as the most popular programming language for the sixth year in a row if you are looking to learn a language that provides more job opportunities java is probably the choice for you python is becoming more and more popular it s been ranked one of the top five most popular languages and was recently listed number two python is a general purpose language the great thing about python is you don t have to be a cs expert to code in it it s a perfect language for beginners python was one of the first language i learned it took me a little longer to pick it up than sql but it wasn t hard you can use python to build pretty much anything below are some examples game development iot machine learning software development web development because of everything you can do with python you have a wide range of job opportunities if you know this language i m not surprised that this language is used by most businesses it s great for almost any industry in fact the results of stackoverflows developer survey ranked python as the most wanted language of even if you don t want to work primarily with python in the long run it s a worth learning the fundamentals of the language in the end i don t just believe that python is one of the best programming languages to learn for i believe that python could be the language of the future this article has more on the best languages to learn']

you are the developer and you are not well versed in a d app like blender or how to draw and more so draw enough frames to animate do you find a friend and hope for the best hire someone or do the best you can and hope its good enough hire someone freelance if you can t do it there s a lot of sites that have royalty free models then there s also plenty who advertise their services in addition more you know the more money you save since you can do it yourself gt how do you determine music sound fx create them and work them in to playing contextually e g music speeds up or intensifies as a round nears the end etc it s pretty easy to change the playback rate of music your game engine is going to determine how you do this but it could be anything from setting an option on your audio source to a very low level change where you get your notes at different cpu clock rates remember music is played in discrete increments it s not continuous so you just play more notes per second gt how do you ensure a very accurate hit collision zone e g if you have a circle that is touching a square how do you ensure the edge of the circle touching some part of the square is a hit back in the day years ago it was basically a square box hit test computers were not fast enough to do complicated geometric hit tests with polygons etc or if they did it was a very basic d line art sort of game it s still the same today usually you have your geometry but then you also have much simpler collision geometry depending on d vs d it s fastest to break everything into square box or circle sphere colliders but there are also capsules cylinders mesh and others there s different formulas for each collider and they all get checked in most circumstances it s best to stay away from mesh colliders and just put a bunch of different simpler colliders on an object box colliders are incredibly fast to compute and box colliders can be faster to check than a single mesh collider gt ai is there an engine that you use or do you write your own ai specific to your type of game for example i am a fan on the mobile anyway of defense like games you place towers whatever along a path and then hope you did it well enough to defend lots of variations on this though but turrets missiles emp pulses etc they all need to target track the enemy based on durations turns etc an ai that just knows where the enemy is because you are the coder after all can simply target the enemy perfectly but a realistic ai cant see around corners for example or based on any number of situations circumstances cant reach attack so how do you figure out this sort of code along the same lines d shooter games like destiny etc the enemies are usually pretty dumb in these games but they still do a pretty good job of targeting you sometimes unfairly too accurate missing sometimes hitting others etc obviously there is some random generator work going on here to determine if their many bullets hit or miss but along with that they are looking at you hiding behind things dodging etc and often it is different depending on the enemy this is actually an offshoot of the same math behind collisions when determining point collisions you give an object a field of view and a detection zone for each object in the detection zone again circle or square colliders work best you can fire a ray from the object to the target and if the ray doesn t intersect anything that would block the field of view you know you have a clear shot engines like unity and unreal have all the functions for this built in but it s relatively simple to write your own other parts of ai will have their own requirements it s usually just a bunch of different functions that all interact with each other gt given todays computer speeds even on mobile but do game engines make this relatively easy today like unity or is there still a lot of work a developer has to do inside the game loop for all these sub systems that make up the game hardware has gotten faster while the formulas are the same computational complexity as they were years ago the end result is that you can do far more computations today as long as you re using the proper formulas and optimizations code isn t really a bottleneck anymore most of your frame budget tends to go to rendering unity or any other engine will handle a lot of this stuff automatically but it s still something you need to be aware of because an inefficiently written script can destroy your performance edit for a little more detail on unity the way unity works is that it has a script execution order first all of your scripts run in a certain order usually the order doesn t matter too much but the order of some predefined functions does matter i ve probably got the order on a couple of these wrong but all awake functions run then start then earlyupdate then update then coroutines then fixedupdate if the interval is correct then lateupdate after all scripts run then rendering for the frame happens once rendering is complete it moves to the next frame also something you may not be aware of is that unity is single threaded so that has some impact on it s efficiency', 'gt how do you deal with graphics and animation e g if you are the developer and you are not well versed in a d app like blender or how to draw and more so draw enough frames to animate do you find a friend and hope for the best hire someone or do the best you can and hope its good enough all of those options are doable pick the one that best fits your project more important than having good art is having unique and consistent art especially as an indie dev lots of successful games have used very simple aesthetics gt how do you determine music sound fx create them and work them in to playing contextually e g music speeds up or intensifies as a round nears the end etc there are a number of ways to handle this but one common way is to have multiple tracks in the music that fade in or out depending on the current situation the player is in gt how do you ensure a very accurate hit collision zone e g if you have a circle that is touching a square how do you ensure the edge of the circle touching some part of the square is a hit back in the day years ago it was basically a square box hit test computers were not fast enough to do complicated geometric hit tests with polygons etc or if they did it was a very basic d line art sort of game computers can easily handle fairly complex collision shapes you also might be overestimating the complexity of hit boxes needed in modern games a more specific answer to this question would require more information about the game itself gt ai is there an engine that you use or do you write your own ai specific to your type of game game ai is very specific to the game being designed but there are a number of common techniques that ai programmers rely on for example you can define the ai s behavior as a set of goals where each goal has a current priority score based on the current game state and the ai just pursues whichever goal currently has the highest priority each goal can contain sub goals and some of those can contain sub goals until down at the lowest level you have a list of atomic tasks like move to position x fire at entity y etc gt in a fps you have of a second to cycle through every enemy on the map determine its next th slice of whatever on top of sound music player movements background graphics etc etc is there still a lot of work a developer has to do inside the game loop for all these sub systems that make up the game unless you re making a fighting game your game should never depend on a specific frame rate timing in game engines is actually a more interesting problem than novice game Name: 4_internship_summer_internships_intern
Representative_Docs: ['gt if software engineering internships are so competitive why is programming one of the most in demand fields at the moment how can something be extremely in demand yet very competitive there seems to be a serious disconnect here internships are different internships aren t there because the company needs to hire someone right now they re there because the company wants to invest in the future if i hire an intern now that intern might be a really good employee by the time they graduate a year or two from now also job openings happen all year round but most internships all happen at the same time in the summer so you have lots of students all competing for a limited number of positions all at the same time that leads to a lot more applicants for each opening gt regarding the interesting stories bit again you re hiring an intern for three months not a permanent employee it shouldn t be a requirement he prove entertaining to get the job i think you re reading too much into it i m not asking for entertainment i m asking for complete sentences i see a lot of resumes that are literally just lists of bullet points a list of classes you took a list of languages you learned a list of frameworks you ve used that s it even if you haven t done anything even remotely extra at least you could talk about a final project for a programming class something like this for our final project for our programming class we had to make an ai to play a game i decided to implement risk because it s my favorite board game my teacher said it d be harder to implement because it involves dice rolls rather than pure strategy but i read a chapter on monte carlo simulations and used that to have the computer pick the best move the end result was still easy to beat but it made moves that were a lot better than random is that more realistic gt also an intern is not a temp worker legally speaking anything beyond basic training that doesn t replace the work of an ordinary employee is against the law he shouldn t be assigned important projects to complete on his own or any projects at all for that matter that will be then used by your company in any strictly non evaluatory capacity that s true if it s an unpaid internship for a paid internship it s totally fine for the company to assign important projects what better way to learn gt interns are not free or cheap labor for a company i know they re often treated as such but they re just there to learn and should not be contributing to the company in such a manner exactly the opposite interns are paid almost as much as a junior employee they take a lot of extra effort to mentor and train and just when they start to get good they leave but it s totally worth it because it s fun and rewarding and some of the interns return to become employees even if only return those are totally worth it gt anyway thank you for your response and telling me the reasoning behind your decisions and sorry for sounding like a prick this topic really gets my goat for some reason if that s the proper expression i ve enjoyed the discussion i totally understand your point of view i totally understand that there are companies out there that have ridiculous hiring requirements and mistreat interns that totally sucks but i think there s a big difference between unreasonable requirements for no reason and companies that are selective because they get a lot of applicants and only have a certain number of openings i m trying to give advice for how to get a good internship knowing there are a lot of exchange program for a few st year master students already planned on going for a master s to go to mit for a summer internship this would be a pretty big deal and even though i really don t think my grades are good enough i m gonna apply anyway next year with this in mind i was thinking of applying for a research internship at tech consultancy company to further my chances i ve already contacted a company and they should get back to me early next week area would be data science i m wondering if this is worth a shot would my application be more attractive with an undergrad research position under my belt considering undergrad research isn t common here another thing to keep in mind is that there s a preselection that s done by my faculty as of now my resume has one serious internship and undergrad ta experience as well as the usual fluff', 'while there has been a lot of talks about how one doesn t need a phd to do deep learning ai ml it seems the basic requirement is still a masters degree which is something i don t have as an undergrad who s interested in research and came close to publishing a paper at one of the top conferences what should i do to get a good internship in this area while the big companies offer ai internships for bachelor students it s under the condition that the student is in his her final year will be returning to a graduate program and has published at a top conference before working towards this but realistically speaking this might be a bit prohibitive since many phd students even at top universities do not publish successfully at these conferences moreover while in swe it is workable to achieve a good internship so long as one practice the standard leetcode questions research is much more variable as it depends a lot on funding and people available at your institution as it turns out i m in one of the top universities but nearly all the good researchers in my university have been poached away by the industry and ironically almost no one from the university managed to publish at top conferences like nips icml cvpr these past few years despite being a top university i m now mostly performing research part time and on my own effort with zero funding holding a full time job to meet survival needs but my true interest lies in research which i feel is most intellectually challenging and fun to work towards my goal i ve had done some full time research before attending university and implemented several architectures from papers which are somewhat well received on github i can t get a pure ai ml internship and so the closest one i could get is a data science role in a tech unicorn startup miraculously which allows me to do some form of research application but with no prospect of publishing of course it pays somewhat alright for me to survive and do part time research i could ve gone to do full time research during this summer but as someone wanting to go into industry research and having had only research experiences and no industry experience i thought taking the ds role was the most logical path in fact it was quite enlightening to see that even the company i work for rejects people on being too academic and not knowing how to apply his her research skill to industry so i thought the ds role might help bridge the gap between academia and industry due to strict university regulations i m unable to hold a research position during term time which i find is the most prohibitive to doing research when almost every other university in the world allows undergraduates to do precisely that for reference because of this i m not even allowed to request for some datasets to work on because i m not funded or do not have a pi i honestly want to get into this field but i think it is almost impossible to do so without spending some exorbitant amount on a masters having held a full time research role for a while before university to give some context i am quite an old student and started university quite a few years late i don t think i lack the capacity to do research but the circumstances have made it so that it s difficult for me to work towards a research career in the industry with just a bachelors i m aiming for mostly roles like a research engineer not to mention that technically i still don t have a bachelors while the big tech companies have easier internship programs for first and second year students it seems that there are none for the research ai ml field it s either that as a bachelors i must already excel tremendously by publishing papers at top conferences which is hard given my circumstances although i m not giving up or i must have at least a masters or phd with a good thesis to get into those positions there doesn t seem to be an alternative path for people who fit into my category are there any other pathways that one could try out as it seems most interesting applied problems are worked by people within the industry especially those with direct impact e g robotics for rehabilitative uses which interests me the most i think the industry has the funding to do this kind of work and commercialize it to make the work accessible to people for impact to happen which is why i m looking mainly at industry at the moment for academia i don t think it s likely i could reach that level of contribution scale of impact i want to have without a phd at the minimum and the present trend for the field is for academic researchers to move into industry anyway so i don t think it makes sense to go to academia only then to go into industry again after many years within the industry there are options big tech research departments e g fair google brain deepmind etc or startups for startups there are just so many that are simply looking for people to hack together some tensorflow solutions and the scale is too small to work on any meaningful problems not to mention the ones that have responded to my emails are looking for remote and unpaid interns as for the rest i believe i got filtered out on the basis of still being an undergrad i think big tech firms are more lenient in the academic requirements but they require some noteworthy evidence good papers to prove one s worth there seem to be some undergrads who have had no such publications but have gotten an internship at these places still e g google brain but how did they achieve that i ve kind of followed these people and saw that they got publications after working in these places which i think is reasonable i m not yet in my final year of studies so what can i do now to get a career in industry research research engineering']

Name: 5_machine_machine learning_learning_spam
Representative_Docs: ['gt how do they learn the mistakes they make that s actually fairly simple i shall present to you naive bayes machine learning algorithm commonly used in detecting spam and ham emails so first imagine you have a set of emails one says buy car now for xmas best offer second is hey matt wanna play tonight last is best wishes for xmas season your grandma now we also know labels for these emails first is spam second and third are ham now we go over email and extract words from it buy spam car spam now spam for spam xmas spam spam next we go to email hey ham matt ham wanna ham play ham tonight ham and finally to mail best ham wishes ham for ham spam look at email to see where for came from xmas ham spam season ham your ham grandma ham now we encounter a new unknown email with contexts say only tonight special offer for xmas buy now so we take each known word into our database tonight for xmas buy now and use it to assign a value to an email in this case word tonight indicates that it s legit but buy and now are both heavily pointing towards spam so confidence that this email is ham would be meaning it s chance of being spam now if you categorized this email manually you would get more words in your dictionary eventually after having hundreds of them you end up with a robust system that can tell which words are particularly strong towards indicating ham eg your name in it and spam buy offer etc system can even categorize automatically on what it already knows and then add words from a given mail to it s database so here you go a simple yet effective machine learning system of course this one is one of the simpler ones but the general rule towards machine learning is the same gradual improvement over time as more and more data is available and you extract specific features from said data as for learning from the mistakes i will simplify by a lot go visit for actual course on this and proper details explanations basically you start from a specific output data and given labels you also prepare a list of features so in this case above our features could be individual words from email and it could be some extra data like sender s email meta tags etc a feature could be word grandma is in an email with a value no or yes now if we have only one feature word grandma then a system can be defined for instance as x theta prediction x is our or for a word grandma theta is what we want our system to adjust automatically prediction is spam or ham so this system would start from setting theta to say and see that it s overall accuracy is since it always says its spam as anything then it tries raising it to say and sees it improved to then it tries it will eventually find a point where accuracy can no longer be improved to understand how it does so you will need to understand what are derivatives and gradients however but long story short you are trying to decrease an error in your predictions in each iteration so resulting function ends up being as accurate as possible', 'but muh machine learning', 'what s machine learning']

Name: 7_masters_ms_cs_master
Representative_Docs: ['if you do a phd in cs make sure they are covering you tuition and you should get a stipend some masters degrees will also do this at least for thesis based masters but not all do especially for professional masters degrees i think you can wait until after the phd to pay off the debts and for a cs phd you should be making enough to cover the debts after you graduate although more debt generally isn t ideal i would mostly suggest if you have to pay for a full masters yourself make sure that people normally graduate on time some programs have a tendency to drag on look up imposters syndrome many people in graduate programs have it to some extent while your cs knowledge may not be as good as other students at the phd level cs has a lot of overlap with applied math so your math physics knowledge should be very helpful i would think ai research would greatly benefit from a strong math background the best benefit i got out of my masters was more detailed research experience and improved knowledge in the research area that i focused on for my thesis a masters won t make you an expert the way a phd will but it will give you much more knowledge in cs and in particular ai assuming that is what you focus on it will give you some breadth but probably not as much as a bachelors in cs would the more specific you are the better you can choose a school that is a good fit for you i would suggest at least knowing what area of ai you want to focus on natural language processing computer vision etc if you want to do something really specific there may only be a few schools doing research on that area if you have less of an idea of what you want to research it can be helpful to go to a school with more professors in that area so that you have more options on who to work with doing summer internships in industry should help with this at least at my school a lot of graduate students do internships at places like google facebook etc especially for ai this should help you get some industry experience help those businesses get to know you and allow you to get advice from the people working there on what you need to do to get a job at a place like that after you graduate one other thing i would suggest doing to help decide between a phd and masters is looking at the type of jobs you would like to get after finishing graduate school and seeing if they require a phd or not if you don t need a phd to get the jobs you want it is probably better to just get a masters', 'gt meng is essentially an extension of a bachelors in canada us it just includes coursework and sometimes a project it s generally not seen as an appropriate preparation for a phd and is used as a terminal degree to learn a bit more about your field an ms in canada us includes some coursework but the main component is a research thesis project an ms is the degree you get before a phd or sometimes its also used as a terminal degree but you can do both i might be wrong but from what you re describing the meng is basically the same here an integrated masters essentially employers respect it as much as an msc as far as i m aware but yea who knows gt if you are like sure what you want to do i don t see any reason not to jump right in again you might be surprised about what phd research is really like but chances are if you re that committed to the field it is probably the one you want to be in i hope so honestly you know how impressionable we are at younger ages i m still and i feel like my mentality changes month by month so i m scared of a phd commitment but i have to decide within the next months so i m trying my best to do as much research as i can and make up my mind gt i think you re a bit more likely to get into programs if you already have your masters shows you can be successful at the graduate level already but i d say the difference isn t dramatic enough to warrant doing a masters for solely that reason exactly what i was thinking perhaps an msc in a relevant ish field would give me the edge but i can t afford an msc so it s either meng or bust which isn t a research oriented masters as you outlined before gt i d say the main benefit of doing a masters is exposure to your research field before you jump into your phd you might have a general idea of what you want to do but ultimately you have no way of knowing specific details until you start working in the field i had a pretty good idea of what i was getting into with my ms field but there are still details i wasn t aware even existed when i was applying to programs gt for example you might know you want to work on machine learning applications in robotics then maybe your ms thesis is working on machine learning in computer vision or something but then along the way you learn the knitty gritty details of computer vision don t really appeal to you but a side project you did for one of your courses on adaptive control systems for robotics was really interesting and you decide you want to pursue that for your phd so overall you never deviated from wanting to do machine learning in robotics but the sub field in that umbrella might change after you do an ms thanks so much for this piece of input it really does offer a different perspective i ll try digest it and see where that leaves me yea phds here are shorter in scotland a beng is years meng is but iirc a beng in england is years yes', 'gt oh i see are mengs not as appreciated in the us or what s up meng is essentially an extension of a bachelors in canada us it just includes coursework and sometimes a project it s generally not seen as an appropriate preparation for a phd and is used as a terminal degree to learn a bit more about your field an ms in canada us includes some coursework but the main component is a research thesis project an ms is the degree you get before a phd or sometimes its also used as a terminal degree but you can do both gt i feel as though i know what i want to do my phd in if you are like sure what you want to do i don t see any reason not to jump right in again you might be surprised about what phd research is really like but chances are if you re that committed to the field it is probably the one you want to be in gt are there any explicit benefits for having a masters am i less likely to get into a program with a beng only i think you re a bit more likely to get into programs if you already have your masters shows you can be successful at the graduate level already but i d say the difference isn t dramatic enough to warrant doing a masters for solely that reason i d say the main benefit of doing a masters is exposure to your research field before you jump into your phd you might have a general idea of what you want to do but ultimately you have no way of knowing specific details until you start working in the field i had a pretty good idea of what i was getting into with my ms field but there are still details i wasn t aware even existed when i was applying to programs for example you might know you want to work on machine learning applications in robotics then maybe your ms thesis is working on machine learning in computer vision or something but then along the way you learn the knitty gritty details of computer vision don t really appeal to you but a side project you did for one of your courses on adaptive control systems for robotics was really interesting and you decide you want to pursue that for your phd so overall you never deviated from wanting to do machine learning in robotics but the sub field in that umbrella might change after you do an ms but again i don t think its a bad idea at all to go straight into a phd if you are fairly sure on what you want to do i m just giving you an alternate perspective on why choosing to do a masters before might be appealing gt here it s only ish years probably closer to so i m quite keen on just getting it done is it really only ish years out of beng you finish your beng after your rd year of full time studies right that sounds extremely short to me but i don t really know everything about the uk sstem']

Name: 8_schools_school_cs_program
Representative_Docs: ['tl dr i m going back to school for an ms in computer science degree to explore a potential career in academia and also give myself more time to mature for a better chance of doing well in industry i m trying to decide between a fully funded offer at colorado state university versus an offer at georgia tech with no funding that i had a better gut feel about ignoring the cost i m trying my best to choose a school while minimizing my regrets and i thought people here might have advice that would help me decide as background i got my ba in computer science from a small liberal arts college two years ago and started working the summer after graduation at a large software company i found the learning curve overwhelming and decided my interests and personality were probably more suited to academia so i quit last summer after working there for a year since then i ve devoted the past year to investigating applying to and visiting grad schools i decided i wasn t ready to commit to years in grad school so i applied strictly to ms programs with the hope that i could easily pivot back to industry or on to a ph d depending on how my interests evolve over the next two years now georgia tech in atlanta ga and colorado state university in fort collins co are my top two picks among the schools i ve been accepted to csu is offering me funding via a ta position which would fully cover tuition and provide an month stipend while georgia tech is not guaranteeing any funding however after visiting both schools in person this month i feel like i d be slightly happier at georgia tech if money wasn t an issue gatech feels like a better fit in terms of my research interests especially computer graphics and as a top cs university in the us i m more optimistic about the job prospects and networking opportunities they would provide i was also impressed by the beautiful weather during my visit this month after flying down from wisconsin the city and campus felt like a botanical garden by comparison and i felt like the warm climate would be good for my mental health i don t feel like i d be making a bad choice with csu even though the school didn t grab me the way gatech did i grew up about an hour away from fort collins so i m more familiar with the area csu doesn t offer any graduate research in graphics but i am interested in their computer vision research group and the small group of cs faculty and students seemed close knit the weather in fort collins is generally nice but i had the misfortune of visiting during a spring snowstorm which i think subconsciously turned me off of the school a little bit would it be better to take my funded offer at csu or try to make georgia tech work they give funding priority to ph d students but the professors i talked to said there s a minority of ms students who get ta or ra positions i have several semesters of experience grading assignments for cs classes as an undergrad as well as experience tutoring students one on one which i m hoping will make me a more attractive ta candidate i d most likely need to take out student loans which i didn t need to do for undergrad but i m hoping my job prospects after graduation would be good enough that paying the loans back wouldn t be too big of a strain thank you all for any advice', 'two weeks ago i made this post asking if it was worth going first ignored these us schools due to their outrageous price my wealthy aunt is offering to pay a portion of my education if i go south no matter where i attend the price will be comparable offers i have the edge in terms of recruiting and networking most students who i would go to school with will likely end up in california the weather and school environment will be nice too though that would be secondary on the other hand despite how f visa students have work authorization employers still discriminate in hiring them why should i hire this kid who s gonna stay here for years when i can just hire a citizen in addition having someone pay for a part of education can be risky if my aunt undergoes financial trouble or just doesn t want to fund me anymore it s over for me then the majors i was accepted at ucla and ucsd to might pose a problem ucla math of computation means a lot more math classes than cs classes ucsd data science is really new and narrows my skillset to data science machine learning which often includes a master s which at that point i likely cannot afford my thoughts on waterloo from what i ve heard silicon valley recruiters love waterloo so going to the us and spending all that money isn t worth it i ll have two years of work experience because of co op and since i m a canadian citizen i ll have access to various loans scholarships and internships that i would not have if i were to go to the us one thing is though i really have no evidence that waterloo is a good cs school other than from a few news articles and anecdotal evidence from reddit even on rankings it s pretty low i know it has to do with research but research seems to be correlated with school quality like how berkeley cmu mit and stanford are all ranked high final thoughts i know i ll always wonder what if no matter where i go but i don t want to think back about how dumb i was in high school to have chosen x school when i had an offer from y school any insights opinions facts or anything about my choice am i crazy for turning down some california schools for waterloo cs', 'i went to a lac so disclaimer here i now work at a unicorn got the job in the fall of my senior year had a lot of other offers at big and stuff too i also hustle a lot so it might skew my perception but you need hustle even if you go to stanford so here are my opinions good schools are good schools imo if you get into a top or top liberal arts school their cs is not going to be bad more importantly your peers will be smart which i think is a bigger factor than what classes are available if you go to cmu or mit you might have more choices for electives but imo none of those extra cool classes are things you cannot learn online by yourself or in a small group with some friends if you won t do that you probably won t do that well in person anyway my average cs class size was probably we had everything from computer vision to ai to ml to systems to os to contrary to many comments the benefits of good small lacs is the recruiting imo if you go to a good liberal arts school williams claremont colleges amherst etc all the companies will come recruit i went to the claremont colleges and everyone Name: 10_math_calculus_algebra_mathematics
Representative_Docs: ['at my school they only require discrete math but if you want to get into serious concepts like machine learning you ll want to get through linear algebra which has calculus and vector calculus as a prerequisite', 'well already got plenty of answers but i ll chip in something too so most of the time no you don t need math however if you want to get into more complex programming or want to learn about subjects such as graphics programming cryptography machine learning data analysis language design et cetera then yes some math is needed perhaps the most important subject is linear algebra especially vector and matrix math a branch of mathematics that one specifically benefits from is discrete mathematics that deals with finite sets and discretely increasing decreasing numbers as opposed to infinite sets and continuous real numbers graph theory set theory and type theory would be the most useful generic subjects combinatorial geometry and topology for graphics information theory combinatorics probability and number theory for cryptography luckily enough if you were interested in studying math a lot of universities and other academies offer special intermediate tier courses covering interesting topics from discrete mathematics as a whole usually these courses are specifically aimed for computer science students and programmers before taking such a course you may wish to take a course in logic or mathematical logic or like introduction to mathematics or something like that that might cover some basics and refresh on the basic symbols used that said you ll also learn some mathematics by just programming it s not necessary to know mathematics when going into programming even though it does certainly help', 'as others said most programmers don t need a lot of math but some programmers do anyone working on ai machine learning scientific programming d games or anything with video graphics or sound uses math fairly often in addition people who work on advanced algorithms and data structures use a lot of math to work with big data or to make really fast search engines databases and things like that if you make simple web sites and apps you might not use much math separately from that remember that computer science is not just programming it s the theory behind how computers work for some of that theory you need to use math to analyze things calculus is not one of the more important math subjects for computer science however calculus is a good example of a college level math course if you can t make it through calculus you might struggle with other math taught in c s even though it s not the same as calculus']

Name: 11_ml_hype_ai ml_people
Representative_Docs: ['my biggest gripe with this whole sub is the well intentioned feel good and ultimately lazy advice that people give out very very few people actually do true ai ml so everyone else is just speculating about what it s like people assume that it s interesting for no other reason than pop culture or medium articles and tell everyone who s vaguely interested in math to go into ai ml because they have no idea what they re talking about there was actually a real ai ml guy mit phd contracted by the dod at hr who made a post about what real ai ml is like and what fake ai ml is like e g adjusting some parameters and writing python scripts he was downvoted to hell and everyone said that he was gatekeeping guess what ai ml is just a discipline of math like any other mathematical discipline you need a graduate to degree to conduct serious research and even then it s a crap shoot', 'gt really be more sustained job growth than other areas like data ml and big data are the primary reasons data is seeing sustained job growth so i m not sure what this means gt in years or whenever will the ml hype die out sure but plenty of places will still use it effectively it s just that a lot more places want to capture the lightning in the bottle when they have need for ml or lack the requisite infrastructure data to utilize it gt will it be the premier career path for software engineers software engineers will be building software not ml algos gt i got offered machine learning internships this summer from avg companies like ibm when i hardly know what a neural network is there s a lot of carrot on a stick positions that dangle the opportunity to maybe support ml initiatives once you get there it s mostly excel work or building a training data set ie data munging these are important work but not generally what candidates have in mind when they think of ml ultimately ml is not as glamorous as media makes it sound so there is major saturation for entry level roles and then a very easy climb for people already inside the funnel who decide to stay in and have the talent patience for it i don t know what you mean by good job prospects but there will Name: 12_course_machine learning_machine_learning
Representative_Docs: ['i would say you should attend andrew ng on coursera before you attend this course let me warn you that the course covers concepts in quite detail the course is not only hands on but also covers a great deal of mathematics behind the concepts i enjoyed the lectures but at the same time i have to put and extra effort to get hold of math concepts the course also lists other useful material that you can read through in my opinion this is a must for embarking your journey on machine learning however i am putting other links that you can explore google machine learning crash course google is pushing big time for ai and machine learning it would be worth exploring these tutorials however i think you must have some knowledge of machine learning before attending the course else you will end up having knowledge gaps edx micromasters program edx is a great platform to enhance your skills you can pick up micromasters course of your choice from any of the reputed institution i have attended few good course on edx and i can vouch for the platform any date i hope that helps', 'you could find andrew ng s coursera he is go to guy for machine learning', 'this is a famous course and is the course that put coursera on the map it tells you everything you need to know about machine learning from the basics to the very beginning of the advanced you ll learn the core concepts of what machine learning is what kinds of problems it can solve and how to apply it given your problem you ll learn about both regression and classification supervised and unsupervised learning and several prominent ml algorithms as well as vital concepts like cost functions gradient descent and broader concepts useful for implementation such as vectorizing for data representations i can t praise this course enough it gives you all the tools you need to prepare for more advanced learning and practice in all things ml you can come from virtually nothing too all that s required is around a high school senior s level of math arguably though a background in linear algebra is required for the course linear algebra is central to ml and personally i was lucky enough to have taken an extensive intro course in linalg back in college so i knew the concepts of matrix multiplication and transposition but even if you haven t andrew ng provides an optional set of videos at the end of the first week of this course and goes through all the concepts of linear algebra that you ll need for machine learning as for neural networks this intro course only covers very basic nns for a couple weeks in the middle of it in the nn assignments in this course a pre built model is given to you with only one hidden layer in the net the goal is to basically define all the auxiliary functions required for the nn to process its node and one layer and how to basically navigate through a layer from input to output so unfortunately the neural network section of this course isn t very robust but i highly recommend knowing this first before moving onto ng s neural network courses that are robust and show fully how to forward and backprop through many hidden layers instead of just one every other algorithm including the most popular among ml practitioners like linear regression logistic regression k means clustering and support vector machines are all brilliantly covered just in this course and you ll get sufficient knowledge in all of them for the time being these algorithms are still dominant in real world ml however this is likely to change within the next decade or so since neural nets and deep learning are experiencing a huge resurgence after being basically relegated to university researchers for so long so take that one course i linked and at the end see if you like machine learning and decide where you want to go next in your machine learning learning if it s neural networks you re after go to andrew ng s deep learning specialization if it s the more currently practiced standard algorithms you want for immediate industry applications try the university of washington machine learning specialization i myself am going through the deep learning spec by ng on course of currently but am also very interested in the uw spec and plan to take that once i have enough spare time to do so i hope that helps let me know if you need anything else oh and do check out r machinelearning as well as r mlquestions where people will help you on some of the more basic concepts and be patient with you while doing it']
