Representative_Docs: ['like pogtastic said you need to get that doc into text and the tools he suggested will help if the pdf has the text within it however if the pdf is a static image those words exist only as images you re still not out of luck though you can convert the pdf s to a tiff convert density your pdf depth strip background white alpha off your tiff then you can have tesseract an ocr optical character recognition program convert the image of the letters into text now with the matching of the words if they are exact or close to exact word matches this is easy and can be done through regex a grammar that allows you to define textual patterns and if all of this is true hell you could automate your job in bash however if the language can sometimes be squirrelly you re going to have to use some kind of machine learning nlp natural language processing stuff and that route is complex there be dragons', 'image recognition based systems are both hard to run in terms of computing power needed and hard to code doubly so if you need to rely on images that can change that being said you can look into opencv library for python it does offer good tools for working with video feed and by extension by graphics generated with your video card gt do i have to use image processing library and make it read the text on the screen kinda although in practice you need to first you need to find a location where text is like a rectangle then you remove all background etc and turn whole thing into greyscale if possible easier to work with colours than with million then you binarize remaining image so it s pure black text on a white background with no noise or grays gradients shadows basically take a pixel and if average of it s colours is exceeding x eg then turn it black otherwise turn it fully white then you tokenize said image turning it into areas containing individual characters that can be analyzed separately hint spacing between characters is fairly uniform you basically look for white spacing separating characters and only then can you shove it through an ocr algorithm which hopefully you take off existing project and not make your own although that too is possible with good accuracy if you know font and it s size that will be used but if you don t and need something generic then it s time to deploy machine learning and read scientific papers on this with decent accuracy that s the gist at the very least it is much easier to just read process memory after all strings to display score and whether or not an answer was correct incorrect must be stored in some kind of variable heck you often don t even need to know how to code to play with these cheat engine for instance lets you accomplish this relatively easily sure you still need to do your research and see what memory sectors change and when what s the text encoding and whatnot but it s way simpler than trying to analyze actual image', 'my two bit advice from a guy Name: 85_game_engine_td_gameplay
Representative_Docs: ['tldr in topic title i work at a large university in the us where we ve made various internal and external applications for staff and students my experience includes using various web dev technologies databases working on projects throughout various stages of the production life cycle and all the things associated with a typical web development job my main programming language is javascript but i ve taken traditional programming classes and over the years i feel like i ve become a better programmer generally speaking i don t think learning something like c c more in depth would be a problem for me i know some of it already however i don t have a degree i haven t taken any math classes for several years and i don t know anything about d programming or game development platforms like unity i ve done some research recently and i ve heard horror stories about the game dev industry but i m not concerned about that i ve heard the same things about web dev and i enjoy it my main motivation for wanting to create games is because i think it would be interesting and fulfilling to create something people find value in i get the same feeling from what i do now but i ve always been passionate about games and now that i have somewhat related work experience the idea of working for a game studio seems like a real possibility there is the question of what do you want to do as a game developer which i don t really know to be honest because i don t know what it involves i d be open to starting in a qa role where i could work with developers that create the logic ai game design etc again i m very ignorant about what role a developer plays in the overall creation of a video game i just know i enjoy programming and creating things for people given my general programming and development experience what skills can i start to work on so that let s say in to years i could start getting interviews thanks for the advice', 'yo i m a tech art intern for a game company affiliated with major animation studios have done plenty of reading on this you re looking for the technical director td role also called technical artist or technical animator this means creating in house tools for artists within d software packages like maya houdini etc to make their lives easier or to create powerful effects that are near impossible to do manually tds can be in charge of every possible tool but in bigger studios like pixar or lucasfilms tds tend to specialize in one of the following fields lighting shading td shader programming in shader languages like glsl or hlsl making things look rich detailed and textured example creating automatic bone shaders for the skeleton characters in coco rigging td setting up rigs skeletons in character models to enable animation enabling complex rigs bendy stretchy wobbly muscular unusual anatomy creating autorigging tools requires specialized d software knowledge likely maya an eye for human animal anatomy and in extreme cases some matrix math look up finding dory s hank the octopus it took years to rig him correctly crowds td facilitating animations for many characters birds fish bugs humans at once using crowd simulation software massive golaem may require some work with boids algorithms look it up but otherwise largely revolves around massive s node based system and fuzzy logic ai character effects td technical fashionistas simulating hair cloth and fur they consistently face the challenge of managing collisions since hair and cloth will always be in contact with their character in some way coco s skeleton characters had clothing that kept snagging in between their bones prompting the character fx tds to figure out solutions effects td water fire lightning fracturing smoke simulation in maya and houdini largely revolves around houdini s node based system but can also be heavily technical and therefore the best paid just saw an opening that s an hour there s a blink and you ll miss it shot of a fountain in the bee movie that took months to make and kung fu panda s final fingertouch explosion had dozens of iterations as to how the smoke spread out pipeline td creating tools to enable easier flow of data from one production stage to the next automatic file structuring automatic animation sequencers and exporters etc the most technical and least artistic this role is easy to transition into from vanilla cs considered the least glamorous but therefore also the one with frequent vacancies most of the above require good knowledge of python and mel maya embedded language for building maya tools and pyqt for creating user interfaces there s also the much more technical and math heavy graphics software engineering which i m far less qualified to talk about but definitely worth looking into since it s much more directly related to straight cs', 'i was in a similar position a few years ago having a cs background with some programming experience and wanting to break into game development however having successfully done it i obviously have winner s bias or whatever so take my advice with grains of salt for sure games engineering can roughly be compartmentalised into engine graphics shaders physics animation audio utilities asset importers io threading maths etc gameplay character camera controls game rules and flow game mechanics and systems ai ui d particles and sprite animations batching and pooling event hooks platform build platform code that helps makes the rest of the code platform independent build pipeline etc the key to learning is to be iterative small ideas quick turnaround time lots of iterations lots of projects since your rhetoric seems to point towards gameplay i ll focus on that surprisingly you ll actually need to know quite a bit from the engine side too even if you re always working with a third party engine in order to fully appreciate how things work under the hood for example you ll mostly be using vectors and quaternions floats but you should be aware of the matrix representations and transformations that happen under the hood as well there are lots of overlapping parts for instance you need to be proficient in linear algebra because you ll need to move rotate things around and utilise dot cross products to compute projections and normals all the time you ll need to know how when whether to respect ignore frame time game time scale eg for animations you ll need to know how to integrate ui with the rest of the codebase you ll need to keep physics audio input as thoroughly separated concerns yet be able to have a system to easily fire a sound when the player presses jump etc thus i would suggest approach it from both sides on one hand start with a simple mechanic idea build a prototype using the simplest fastest tools you can twine gamemaker unity unreal lumberyard godot etc and learn from all the problems you solve along the way on the other try reading up some literature on game engine architecture and game programming patterns and try making a minuscule d engine say in sfml c as a parallel long term learning project as for how where to outsource work marketing tips and tricks community management etc you will come across all the relevant info in your journey do not worry too much about it now first focus on becoming capable of realising your part of your small scale visions this is why you must not start with a year game plan but instead three weeks you will know when you re ready to embark on your first commercially viable project yourself addressing your questions gt should i even continue learning python if my goal is game development it won t hurt but it is recommended to use a strongly typed compiled language instead c is the industry standard and also the language of unreal engine but is certainly intimidating to start with fortunately unity is built around c which can serve as a precursor to c gt learning to use a game engine properly could take longer than writing the entire game myself and i wouldn t learn a whole lot of programming out of it is it even worth it i think it is worth exploring how far you can go with writing a simple d engine in parallel with working with a rd party engine of your choice even if you never actually want to build one yourself a lot of problems you will encounter there with separation of concerns managing dynamic objects keeping track of time manipulating d transformations resolving collisions etc are very relevant to higher layer gameplay programming too']

Representative_Docs: ['in my field molecular biology masters cost money but phds are paid for and pay you a stipend for also you don t need a ms before starting your phd so i feel very thankful so Name: 94_infosec_ux_interesting_security
Representative_Docs: ['hopefully find a domain that is interesting while you re still in university in my case that domain was computer graphics and game development i now work at a aaa studio frames 'it kinda sounds like some aspects of ux and human computer interaction hci that field involves a combination of knowledge for technology psychology sociology etc and though it s not quite what you advantage of users with policies or shady design practices i think stanford offers a free course on hci here the role of ux designer consultant is currently less of a social scientist and more of a spent managing stuff being less and less technical more and more managerial e g outsourcing amp off shoring operations rd party vendor management systems migration project and programme management as well as some operational management for a highly visible massive communications system and infrastructure career b small business sme consultancy i was feeling disconnected hands off from the technology and also a bit stressed so i requested an exit after months resting i looked around for something and did not like anything available so took the plunge and started a business helping sme s with supply chains tendering and the like i did this for years working with smaller organisations largely outside london i missed the buzz of working in london and though there was an aspect of data processing nothing like what i wanted career c career reboot options amp paths now i m thinking of a career reboot i ve tried a bit of self learning using freecodecamp and been to a few boot camp tasters and been surveying options plan options priorities are get back into full stack development use a week full stack camp to learn html css javascpt react js node js mongodb in a team scrum agile kanban xp set up with cv prep and help to find that new role lean ai ml data science there are several leaning options here some just skim the basics lt hours learning then launch you into weeks of a full time project others spend weeks teaching and developing a portfolio in class i am also interested in cyber security and was thinking of training myself for cissp cism certification which looks reasonably straightforward the easiest option would be to prepare for a project programme managment certification prince and or pmi s pmp i am also interested in the use of blockchain not simply cryptocurrency devops is in demand at the moment lots of jobs pays quite well and speaks to a significant portion of my experience i ve prioritised these in the order that i think there is the highest demand and median pay for the london market through the data science route has a lot of appeal in terms of future prospects and personal interest i did get bored learning html and css on freecodecamp the path to get there seems to be less clear cut than a regular web app development role so options please given my background and the current market place is development the right choice even if ultimately i want to progress to data science i mean it s better that way around do development track first then data science and actually there a lot more demand for the former at the moment anyway cyber security or project managment would be the easiest in terms of barriers to entry interestingly devops is an in demand area too it s something i can also probably move to with a relatively low entry barrier but that sort of operational role is probably not going to be as challenging as the others', 'so i started out as an entry level programmer at a company and spent a year mostly doing bug fixes and very minor change requests for clients on a web application struts jsp hibernate tomcat oracle database jasper reports but half the times me and this other entry level programmer were scratching our heads trying to figure out how the framework works as we didn t we get a whole lot exposure or guidance since the system analyst that supervised us had almost no experience in java only sql and stored procedures neither did we have a programmer analyst on the team that can teach us either eventually the client bought out the source code and created their own internal team so the project manager moved me to a different project with lots of guidance where i spent the next years doing change requests bug fixes on a vb application with very limited exposure to net wish i had looked for a different job by then and not waste time working with a phased out language but the best take away from all this was getting a solid foundation in writing stored procedures i moved on to an engineering firm with that had a software engineering department and hgiher salary than my previous job again not much real development opportunity as the one java backend processing application i was responsible for was already built any kind of real enhancements if any was done by a senior software engineer so i was pretty much just his assistant doing things he didn t want to do like documentation performance testing sql tuning however this was one job i did not regret taking on as there was less hand holding and it provided far more exposure than my last job had to offer as i had to work with many different variations of unix shell scripting a bit of python continuous integration tools on site installation problem investigation got introduced to agile eventually i had to move on again as it was the only way to get a real boost in my salary next job that i am currently working at is more similar to my first job enterprise web application mvc model again except this time i was involved for most the software development life cycle working with struts jquery building a lot of web services to interface with various other internal systems everything has been good so far and i feel more confident than ever as i noticed a drastic boost in my ability to debug isolating problems solid knowledge on javascript making use of advanced java programming techniques however i never feel too comfortable with any job as i feel like technology changes really fast new and better ways of doing things always showing up bigdata deep learning i feel like i need to keep myself updated to stay relevant in this industry and i feel uneasy about continuing my career in developing enterprise web application systems although it is pretty stable this can easily become a thing of the past i have been looking at stuff like angular nodejs react and thinking about becoming a full stack developer in addition i have also considered becoming a mobile application developer as these mobile apps is whats really in these days i have high confidence in my learning abilities and pretty much every job i worked at always had something new for me to pickup here is the problem i ve looked at salary ranges for these job postings and without taking a salary decrease i would definitely be asking for the top end of the range for jobs in ios android development or full stack developer nodejs angular react yet i m questioning how i can keep my salary relatively the same but i don t have any working experience in these specific technologies any advice', 'some of my career background in july i started a youtube channel as a hobby initially but over the next year found some decent success and was approached by youtube to become a partner which i accepted and slowly transitioned into recording and editing videos and growing my subscriber base i ended up selling last september to an agency since the tole of daily videos after nearly years was starting to wear on me in february of i was looking around on the internet for information related to a video i was doing and came across a small farm for sale in bangladesh for a pretty decent rate talking to the owner through a translator i hired i learned most of the issues were and also that he had moved to dhaka several months before following up on that i hired a lawyer to handle the property records search and for the princely sum of around i was the now owner of a failing farm in a very isolated region of bangladesh i ll summarize the next several years here because it could go on for pages and pages but through my youtube money and profits from the farm and other businesses i would acquire over the next and a half years i acquired total businesses covering a huge array of industries and employing people a lot of this is due to how cheap things are in bangladesh and my ability to market to the western market generally i d also travel over there for a few weeks months at a time if something that required my first hand knowledge or was somehow struggling but largely i just trained my managers on business operations and to submit regular reports and it worked for me i could have lived like a king in bangladesh easily but because of the frequent foreigner killings and growing isis threat and blatant human rights violations i settled on staying in the us i was approached by a local group in march and they made an offer to buy my conglomerate because of the growing safety issues and political tension i accepted and am currently in the process of closing the sale my original plan was to just buy a house here in the us and retire take some time off as per the sale agreement i get a large upfront cash amount and then quarterly payments for the next decades the only reason the sale price is so high is because of several products i developed during my time and their wanting to market them to the western audience my largest business my food packaging and processing facility for example has a neural network trained over a few thousand hours to program plr s and make suggestions for improvements for those not in the engineering field a plr technician typically makes around us yr i ve actually developed a ton of other products despite not being formally trained as an engineer but i d rather not go into details since i want to keep this short my issues a few weeks of nothing to do has made me a bit stir crazy i realize now that i need something to occupy my time with so i figured i d start applying for jobs i mean i have several years of business management experience several more as a currently and that surmises a good chunk of all of the employers i ve only even had interview but the latest rejection has kind of gotten to me the hardest i applied as the assistant manager of apple bees through my past business i had the idea of an automated drone delivery system to solve a logistics problem in bangladesh something that wasn t viable there because of power control issues but i had still dumped months into the software and hardware side of things and had a functional product i not only tailored my resume to the position but made it clear in my cover letter that i would gift them the drone delivery system if they hired me and explained everything about it and how it could boost their car side sales spent the hours filling out their personality test and spread sheet business questions and here i am days after the we are pursuing other candidates email with the job posting still up wondering why i can t even get an interview things i have working against me because i ve done most all my business primarily in bangladesh and since currency is not freely exchangeable i have no credit or any financial history in the us i estimate my income and profits for the irs every year but i don t believe that does much for a background check i ve never had my licence the town i grew up in was tiny and everything was walk able so i never saw the point then after my youtube took off i was too busy to really need want to drive anywhere which just continued as i started acquiring businesses i ve never worked for anyone before and the few peers i ve worked with market but things were still growing well without doing so with a few exceptions i m also pretty sure my lack of a college degree isn t helping but i also cant see myself attending i m all for self careerguidance i need advice i still want to work but i want to try and work under someone for a change i would also like to stop paying for insurance out of pocket am i doing anything wrong']

Name: 99_startup_pay_project_machine learning
Representative_Docs: ['woah ok so fullstack development is something that can be picked up relatively quickly and you can create usable products with not much experience this does not apply to machine learning at all especially with minimal support from your team you can t derive actual benefits from machine learning with just technical knowledge alone you have to understand the environment and problem space intimately you have to know what are false positives and what the acceptable threshold for features are i m in a similar position startup our machine learning algorithms were written by from scratch despite coming in with no professional ml experience so now they are hiring another ph d consultant to redo it edit can gt can t', 'i would express to them that machine learning isn t Name: 101_doctor_doctors_physician_replaced
Representative_Docs: ['the profession has one of the highest rates of burnout and suicide you ll be investing probably over k into it and coming out with that much debt that kind of debt is a huge burden you ll be worked like a dog in med school you ll be devoting your entire day to medicine for years after slaving away to studying you ll get to slave away as a resident enjoy the brutal hours and low pay disrespect from patients this kind of stress adds up over time dealing with bullshit insurance companies paying liability insurance with the rise of technology and the changing healthcare climate the profession of medicine will be in for big changes it is possible that much of a doctor s tasks will be replaced by machines ai is improving at a very fast rate the profession will be affected by ai as will all of human society don t believe me check out the progress ai is making right now', 'nobody s going to trust an ai physician until they re really really well tested at diagnosis and proven to be consistently better than humans and getting that experience is likely to be problematic if you have an ai physician and a human physician reviewing the same case and they agree then there s no problem but the ai is redundant on the other hand if they disagree if the ai says it s cancer but the doctor doesn t or vice versa then who is going to trust the ai over the doctor the doctor sure won t accept the ai s output over his her own judgment after all it s the same type of hurdle as with self driving cars we know that ai can outperform humans maybe by a huge margin but we can t figure out how to get there without turning a generation of people into experimental guinea pigs i don t believe that sociologically we are close to solving that problem', 'i should have worded that better definitely not going to completely shift in the next few years but we re already seeing the very beginning stages if i had to guess i d say we ll more noticeably see this shift taking place in or so years conservatively the likelihood of any senior in college matriculating to medical school is about and declining a ton of med schools are seeing an increase in the average age of m s right now at and in my region we re seeing it go closer to im too lazy to look for the sources for these but this is what i ve been told by my univetrsities med school admissions board medical schools only have a limited number of spaces they can allocate to students and a limited amount of funding they can allocate to pay for residency and residency is where the kicker is because if you can t get through residency and you can t practice this essentially makes the number of new physicians a set number each year while the general need for more physicians is only going to continue to rise there is already a monster deficit doctors in this country and fields like geriatrics oncology family pract syrgery and any other field that has to do with caring for more problems the elderly and obese face will continue to rise the sentiments that the dos and pas will take over a bulk of what the mds are doing lies in that is it probably more likely for the bodies that oversee licensing to extend the responsibilities of this position rather than the hospitals see more money to expand their resodency programs personally i agree with this to me it makes more sense to allow the people receiving a very similar education to take on more of these responsibilities as far as ai goes that s all up in the air imo i think it is much more likely we will see ai as an aid rather than a replacement for medicine in our lifetime so in that sense i think we somewhat agree now while it s true that watson was able to diagnose or so of diseases accurately i can t imagine too many people being comfortable with a robot diagnosing them without input from a person aside from that government is slow and with how little anyone in the government understands the internet i don t see any legislation being passed anytime soon that would allow ai to take the reigns think of it this way who do you bkame when the program is wrong the hospital the company that made the software']

Name: 102_ml_engineer_research_positions
Representative_Docs: ['i interned as a machine learning engineering intern at a pretty notable computer vision company after my junior year at that point i had just a handful of ml classes in school under my belt i d say the thing that got me the job was outside projects through hackathons and personal initiative i have friends in a similar position that have gained their ml ai experience from participation in relevant research projects volunteering for profs etc these days there s all kinds of non traditional ways to demonstrate your interest and develop skills in the field without the grad school certification also in terms of what kinds of jobs to apply to early on check out applied machine learning aml or data science teams in general vs more traditional pure ml research teams many tech companies have them in conjunction with their research teams in order to deploy the ml models their research team makes and effectively apply the research they do it s a great place to learn about ml without being expected to know enough to produce research and it s an accessible role for anyone with a bit of experience as a swe an interest in ml most people i know in serious ml roles without a phd usually arrived there after spending some time in aml data teams', 'i m graduating with a bachelors and working as a research engineer in dl ml when i graduate it depends on what you want if you want to be a swe in ai then just keep doing what you re doing until you move around into a ai team they mostly only care about how good of a coder you are if you want to be directly involved in the ai then you gotta go for the research engineer scientist positions based on my interviews and my background here s what i think you need to demonstrate that you have in order to be a research engineer strong grasp of basic ml stats math programming i took a bunch of ml stats math classes in undergrad since you ve graduated you can go for the mooc or read textbooks one thing that bothers me is that most people want to just jump into trying to do the cutting edge stuff without even knowing what linear regression is and even when they learn linear regression they still have such a shallow understanding it s utterly useless if you really want to get into this field start with the ml moocs i also recommend statistical learning mooc or tutorials you find online and then hammer the concepts home with this guide from the ml subreddit or follow the textbooks from this list of recommendations from ucb grad students i remember the first technical round of my interview was basically a math ml stats quiz where i got asked questions like how do you compute this integral what s newton s method what s lda demonstrate experience i did research and a lot of class projects but this one is pretty simple to explain just get your hands dirty with a project in tensorflow pytorch implement an algorithm with your own twist do kaggle competitions do a project for your company one thing i think is important is to understand the broader picture of your projects if you implement a cnn to classify images why did it work why didn t it work what could you do better one of my interviews was a coding project to implement a recently published paper train it and write a research report be useful as a swe you re usually with a team and talk about decisions to make for this software project and everyone is expected to contribute something to the discussions same for research and ai talk with your friends in ai ml get yourself in a room where someone is phd level in ml and be comfortable talking to them and work through toy ml problems i think it s much more intensive than something like a swe scrum things aren t well defined like programming whatever you suggest is usually gonna be wrong but should help steer your team towards a direction for the correct answer i ve experienced this in my labs and my onsite interviews where i had to talk to a bunch of phd scientists about ml topics and work through open ended ml problems i don t think you need a graduate degree to get into ai if you look at research engineer positions they only require a bachelors usually what they mostly care about is the things i posted above and the interviews at least what i got reflected that also think if you re ready to do this type of work it s a different animal than being a regular swe edit michael i jordan has a post on the books you should read to quickly but rigorously get into ml', 'i ve been seeing a lot of posts lately on people wanting to break into the ds ml area so i thought i would make this post i ll break it into three audiences researchers career changers and new grads current students researchers you have background in a quantitative field such as economics math physics etc phd in a stem from a top program track record of publications in conferences and journals basic proficiency in python matlab r or c jobs you should target data scientist machine learning researcher quantitative researcher analyst obstacles you may face weak programming interview skills hard transition from academia to industry fierce competition for coveted research positions e g fair openai deepmind lack of business perspective and ambiguous job responsibilities how you should be job hunting if still in academia try to submit to top ai focused conferences e g nips icml iclr cvpr attend conferences and other relevant meetups these places almost always have industry booths that are dying to hire top talent use your department s network to find openings reach out to others in your cohort who have switched to industry consider applying to larger companies in established industries that will have research labs set up such as banking pharma automotive etc career changers you have professional masters in a quantitative field such as data science financial engineering operations research etc years in experience in industry hopefully working in a technical or data intensive role projects that highlight your familiarity with frameworks such as scipy tensorflow spark xgboost etc intermediate proficiency in python java and c jobs you should target applied data scientist machine learning engineer data engineer quantitative developer obstacles you may face lack of seniority in data science and machine learning unfortunately a m s is like the new b s in that everyone in the area now has one and even top uni s like uc berkeley and georgia tech have cash cow programs in data science that churn out tons of masters students you will be at a disadvantage if you did not do any data science machine learning internships during masters or undergrad how you should be job hunting leverage your domain knowledge from previous work experience and look for data science and machine learning applications search for smaller companies like startups that have looser filters than big n apply for more applied positions instead of research oriented ones do informational interviews with data scientists to see what they hire for find out which interview skills are important for you they vary from normal leetcode project based implementing research papers sql ml theory statistics puzzles depending on the company new grads current students you have bachelors in computer science statistics or math intermediate advanced proficiency in programming relative to the other two groups knowledge of data structures and algorithms i e leetcode knowledge of sql spark hadoop aws projects in machine learning or kaggle competitions jobs you should target data analyst data engineer software engineer in machine learning software engineer in data science obstacles you may face you are looking for that rare sweet spot in data science for entry level new grad roles every data science position you find online will have many applicants with a graduate degree so you will need to stand out somehow or get a referral your lack of work experience will usually disqualify you for senior roles at larger companies and early hires at startups avoid companies that do not have proper data infrastructure pipeline set up they will likely bait and switch you or try to make you into a jack of all trades data person how you should be job hunting consider data science roles at non tech companies in data driven industries like healthcare analytics network with your professors and classmates next to a graduate degree an internship in data science and machine learning is the next best thing research experience is also really good to have and make great talking points in interviews find out which interview skills are important for you they vary from normal leetcode project based implementing research papers sql ml theory statistics puzzles depending on the company get into a big n as a regular software engineer and try to transition to their data science teams hopefully this guide helps and answers some reoccurring questions i see in this subreddit']

Name: 104_exam_exams_french_school
Representative_Docs: ['hi i am a graduate student in data science can anyone share your experience or opinion to join piscine in paris i want to do sort of summer exchange program in and count the piscine at ecole in i passed its online test many articles say it is a novel school and students reviews are very positive even to piscine do you think it is worthier than summer exchange program in conventional universities thanks in advance', 'udemy is currently having a cyber sell and all of their course s are i m currently getting a master s in experimental psychology with a stats and research design certificate my backup plan if i don t get into the phd program i want is to do data science since i have gained experience with r and coding in general i don t know python sql or seo and a lot of the jobs i ve looked at have these down for basic qualifications has anyone taken any of their course s here s the link to their website', 'l cole est un culte mon avis one of my friends convinced me i should put this information about the school out in the open so people can make a more informed decision about it if it s worth their time also when i was researching the school i didn t find many negative reviews this might just be because most of the people that went are there are operating under selection bias and want to give it a good review because they want a post hoc justification for going to a radically different school i m from fl originally and i did my piscine years ago but i still live in europe and have friends that have taken the piscine since after and some that still go to the school so first some back story because of life circumstances i never graduated highschool and had to fend for myself since i was so for about years i worked just saving my money never owned a car or anything so that i could go back to university one day i ve always been into computers and flash games so i was pretty tech savy already but about ish prior i finally decided to pick up programming i had been taken some french courses in the past so i liked the idea of they market themselves as a new innovative meritocratic and free school system but of course there is more than meets the eye and there is some sort of catch according to their marketing material if you just work hard and have passion for tech you will pass the entrance exams assuming you pass the online test to quote one of instructors if you can call them that in their videos you watch during the beginning of the piscine i ve never met anyone who showed up for hours every day who didn t pass the piscine you will see in a moment why that s bullshit so i did some math and figured out that moving to paris and going to was cheaper including living expenses than trying to get into school in my country not to mention i would waste thousands on upgrading and would take about a year of doing unrelated highschool courses before i could even think about applying to a university locally during the piscine you have to show up every day for at least hours for weeks as well as get a decent mark maybe i m a self starter and a bit of an autodidact so i m usually highly motivated and this wasn t a problem for me especially if it meant i finally could get into a real school i averaged hours every single day but then the weird things started to happen that were on a slippery slope of being trolling or discipline to what i consider borderline abuse or at least putting people in positions of power that they were almost encouraged to abuse i talked to almost no actual staff the entire time i was there as they want you to learn everything on your own which i m okay with however all of the few interactions i had with staff were very negative they have a very holier than thou type of attitude which also seems to have rubbed off on some of the full time students and it wasn t like i was asking them about the course work itself it was more like wheres the bathroom or my key card isn t working ect some of the weird things were the exams the first exam they give you no info on what to do and you have no internet access you have to open up a file in command line and access the exam program and if you don t do it within the first minutes you are kicked out of the exam and marked needless to say of the people failed it these next bits are rumors because i didn t observe them directly but was told by some friends during some of the exams they let the campus cat roam the exam rooms if she touches you automatic fail and you have to leave the exam another one was battleship the exam supervisors sit in a room playing battleship and the board corresponds the the exam room if they sink a ship in your area your entire table gets a fail finally my personal experience during the final hour exam i asked a supervisor if i could get fill my water bottle i was permitted and i went i was in there for less than minutes with a few other people who were going to the toilet and was given no restrictions or time limits as i was finishing up i hear someone yell in french is anybody from the exams here i figured it was a cleaner or something and i wasn t sure how to reply exactly in french i figured i was walking out in seconds so it shouldn t be a big deal as i was about to re enter the exam room some staff member who didn t have an id or anything to identify himself other than a staff shirt yelled in french you stop come here i had never seen this guy before nor did i understand why he was stopping me he started berating me in french with the subtext of kicking me out of the exams because i didn t answer him immediately i pretended that i didn t understand him and just said sorry i don t speak french he begrudgingly let me back into the exam room the weird thing was that the supervisor that gave me permission to go was standing beside him the whole time and never chimed in to say anything it was freaking bizarre to say the least finally the exams were over and i waited to hear the results you can see everyones scores during and after the exams and i did pretty well as i was in the top out of about people the results were in all you get is a short email with no information as to why you failed i didn t get in even though they claimed that being in the top was usually good enough and so was showing up everyday i was curious about taking it again since i didn t really have very many options for school at the time and the official website that you login to actually read try again next time when new slots open up however one of my french friends told me that it wasn t possible he sent me a link to a blog post from over a year ago which was on their tumblr that stated they would stop allowing retries at some point in the year i was skeptical that this was even official information as the tumblr blog looked like some default theme that tumblr has and looked almost like something year old would design and this information wasn t in any of their english language material either it was extraordinary unprofessional looking especially for an innovative tech school and their website wasn t even up to date on the ability to retry the piscine again very unprofessional i was very unimpressed you d think that having an up to date website would be a priority for such an organization with that said i had heard that some people snuck their way in and took the exams more than once by swapping their first and last names in the registration forms since they don t keep any copies of your official id until you ve passed the piscine they either don t care or are too incompetent to make sure people don t game the system that way apparently either that or they are being purposely deceptive which is almost worse i mean schools that they are trying to compete with like mit let you apply every year and don t need to lie to people about it to add insult to injury a few friends i knew that got in told me that every single girl that showed up every day got accepted into the school including ones that had very low scores so they have some affirmative action policies for entrance which means it s not a meritocracy one of the girls i knew that got accepted had never programmed in her life before the piscine and handed in about half the amount of assignments as i did i estimated that at least people got accepted who had a lower score than me initially i thought they were super advanced and using some sort of advanced ai algorithm to determine which students pass or fail but the truth is much more banal i heard that the final decisions are made by some random panel of guys who look at some basic info on you for about minutes and decide if you pass or fail and i ve heard what goes on behind these closed doors can be as ridiculous as failing someone because they don t like the way you look in your profile picture there is at least some percentage of students they pass fail at random basically it could be an attempt to obscure the actual selection criteria by introducing random noise at the expense of students but even that s probably being too hopeful this is a huge problem in their system as you randomly get assigned people to check and that check your work as well as random teams for team assignments in these checks they rate you and sometimes write comments the problem is that this might not capture how well you actually work with people as i d say at least of the time or more i was helping out friends in undocumented ways so it s like if you don t work really well with random strangers then you re screwed but it probably doesn t matter that much since the selection criteria seems to be pretty arbitrary anyway if you are a very motivated person and can work on your own might be okay especially if you are a girl and possibly minority of some kind i know there is a lot of students that lose motivation and i knew a few people that once they got in kind of just dicked around and would only spend a couple hours a day there or would spend most of their time on facebook since there is no formal structure meanwhile motivated individuals such as myself get kicked to the curb for bullshit reasons conclusion unless you happen to live near one of the campuses or its various clones and it doesn t involve any major life changes or risks such as relocating i would not recommend it and if you do get in i would get out as soon as you can use it as an opportunity for networking and to get internships that s it even the curriculum isn t really anything groundbreaking or interesting it s pretty much just a bunch coding puzzles assignments and you use google as your text book and professor the few videos they had giving information were pretty sub par and you can find much better high quality youtubers that are much more informative and entertaining to learn from even sites like udacity coursera or udemy are light years ahead of them imo if you want to learn to code i would say spend of your time working on fun personal projects ideally with the potential for income generation taking cheap bootcamps or moocs and another doing networking activities like meetups or competitions although i do have a feeling that the programmer market is starting to become over saturated a bit as it is so easy to learn now days not that there s no work or that it s not a useful skill to have as a secret weapon but that wages are starting to go down a bit and i think s treatment of students is evidence of that if you have any questions about it feel free to ama theres lots of other weird stuff and cult behavior i saw there but for brevity i tried to just keep the highlights as a final note i am now working at a tech startup no thanks to the jerks at or my countries education system that let me fall through the cracks with not even a highschool diploma edit some ers are brigrading the thread who are upset that i m criticizing their school cult i don t have time to address every single one of them but i can assure you i m not lying and even some of the former students are even confirming some of the stuff i was saying to the ones that say i didn t work hard enough or i wasn t sociable enough i m calling bs i made lots of friends and went out of my way to help people including programming noobies both formally and informally but this is one of the problems with their system if you help a lot of friends and work well with your neighbors that is totally undocumented so when they do they final selection you could actually be very good on the social end of things but they can t tell to me the system is set up to favor very extroverted people which ironically reduces the type of diversity that is actually important edit i swear that the sock puppet accounts are real several new accounts with low karma or haven t been used in ages coming to the defense of some school they ve never heard of before haha they keep trying to get me to dox myself too lol edit several more sock puppets have dm d me trying to get me to dox myself here is one with karma after getting mad i wasn t giving into his demands gt i m done talking to iq wanna be programmer for years have fun at your engineering job and don t ever come again to thanks edit some people likely ers are saying everything is just rumors and hearsay it s not to me it seems like is trying to gaslight people so i ll share another anecdote from the school that i witnessed with my own eyes some poor girl broke one of the rules as far as i know it could have been something as minor as having a water bottle on top of the desk you know what her punishment was they humiliated her by making her go around the entire class room maybe school i m not sure asking people when the last time they showered and changed underwear was she had to tally them on a piece of paper i can get cleaning walls and stuff as punishment because it at least serves a purpose other than for humiliation but this was crossing the line imo lots of weird shit like that happens there edit so it appears i now have some definitive proof of sock puppet accounts take a look if you still aren t convinced theses guys are crazy the sock puppet also posted a photo of some guy he thought was me immediately after he thought he figured out my user name but i m supposed to just trust they won t dox me lol and here is another shill trying to dox me i have more in dms too edit in case anyone reading this has any doubt about some of the kooky and political ideas from this school this was posted on one of their facebook pages i was only able to save the direct link to the image as it wouldn t save the facebook page for some reason so they have a photoshoped image of the founder that reads che version as in the argentine marxist revolutionary with me all are free totally normal totally acceptable definitely not culty definitely not aligned politically with marxist ideologies and certainly not making acceptance of students based on these ideas or politically related ones s gt we believe that knowledge is not useful anymore in fact we believe its dangerous nicolas sadirac director and founder of and for those who believe that they never ask for money that s only half true they don t explicitly ask for money but the whole business model is based around the idea that some successful students will donate back to the school gt to make the school self sustaining he xavier niel figures that future alumni will give back to their school source here are some of the connected threads i ve found thread on programming circle jerk thread on r france thread on r drama who would win a techno cult and a legion of hackers or one salty boii s']

finish answer main', 'hey reddit i m a literature professor who conducts research in global literature and the digital humanities and i m currently working on a project involving the alphabet typography and computation in particular i ve spent the last few months working to design and program a recursive alphabet i m posting this here because i could use your help in assessing the uses and theoretical implications of what i m designing and possibly discuss where to take its development from here for any technical experts who find this promising i could especially use help to formally implement it and explore its possibilities first please find attached a rather basic but workable illustration of what i plan to implement more robustly in code proof of concept model i know it might not seem like much at first glance and i of course welcome critique but i hope you ll entertain a few thoughts i have about what i believe are its vast implications for computer vision philosophy of mind language and the algorithmic implementation of artificial intelligence though i m not a specialist in these fields i am here as a literary scholar to offer what i can to help solve interesting important problems and i believe the solution to ai cognition might unexpectedly require literary forms of thinking reading though literal would be more accurate here relatedly please bear in mind that the use of the latin alphabet here is merely for ease of modeling the real interest will entail swapping in other alphabetical sets such as a programming language with self replicating capabilities the recursive alphabet s most distinctive feature is the visual capability of each of its letters to display as either themselves as per normal and as recursive and atomically structured homoglyphs of themselves composed of every letter of the alphabet for example the single a below can at once be represented as a normal a and also as constituted by the entire alphabet at the same time among which exists another a a bcd e f gh ij klmnopqr st uv wx yz this model should make intuitive sense to us because it stands to reason that even in everyday contexts the letter a presupposes the entire alphabet regardless of the absence of all other letters one could even argue that the phrase the letter a is a tautology x over thus what is represented by a holds for b and c etc as well as for each of the a z letter atoms that constitute the larger a and from there for each of the micro letters and macro letters up and down the scale thus a second majorly distinctive feature of this recursive alphabet is that each letter is in fact recursively homoglyphic vertically i e each letter looks like itself at higher and lower scales of observations as well recursively isomorphic horizontally i e the entire alphabet embedded in one letter is identical in both ordinance and substance to the same entire alphabet embedded in a different letter on the same scalar plane basically what we get is an alphabetical structure that is at once consistent complete and endlessly self referential that has a lot of stability because each of the alphabet s characters both constitute and are constituted by all alphabetical characters i have much more to say about the alphabet s formal implementation including how we can visualize it using an interface that allows for recursive zoom in and out of the various scales of alphabet with attention paid to optimizing draw distance at the limits of human perception and pixel display technologies but the more important application as mentioned is not just to visually represent the recursive structure of the english latin alphabet but to conceive of novel programming applications these may possibly proceed in the form of self replicating quines which print out strings of code that are copies simulacra of the program s original source code having spent a lot of time thinking about and modeling this recursive alphabet it seems clear to me that if properly developed a recursive unicode alphabet could very well be the missing link to true artificial general intelligence and from there everything else while i haven t been able to implement this myself due to the limits of my technical expertise i submit for your consideration the possibility that forms of literary thinking might in fact be essential for conceptualizing how we will build true agi as understood via literary theory re structuralism deconstruction etc one could say our human ability to read and therefore have intelligence is founded on being able to relate everything to everything in terms of metaphor the words you re reading right now for instance are just metaphors for things that they aren t e g the word tree actual tree and these words are literally made of letters that are themselves metaphors for more fundamental letters which include for example subatomic particles like higgs bosons or ideal letterforms etc e g a is not a it s just particles in the shape of a also a is not true a because this gt a is not that gt a however now that we ve modeled a recursive alphabet we have a means by which we can show the interchangeability of meanings between and among any letter or set of letters thus we might be able to teach computers to achieve the same nuance of reading consider the following three letters and a by scrutinizing them you will discover the letters are very slightly different indeed in unicode they are not even of the same local alphabet the first is greek the second is cyrillic and the third is latin like the recursive as of my alphabet these characters are also homoglyphs of each other and are sometimes used in idn homograph attacks however an important difference is that the and a homoglyphs are only hard to detect for humans for computers they re as easy to delineate between as we humans are able to delineate between the micro as and macro as of my recursive alphabet model what is happening is that there are perceptual scales in which humans cannot tell any difference between two given letter defined objects while our unicode ocd computers have trouble seeing distinguishing anything except via hardcoded boolean logic though deep learning can train them to just go with it hence the need for my recursive alphabet in order for us to help ai develop and match human level adaptive reading skills i d argue that it will require a well implemented recursive unicode alphabet ai development up until now has been built on alphabet unicode standards that have hyper rigid or else impossibly ambiguous definitions for what a means however the utility of my recursive alphabet applied to unicode is that it would allow our alphabet ai to understand that every a is in fact made up of the entire cast of other unicode characters this more sophisticated understanding of the recursive morphology of the alphabet will give a i the ability to observe letters at a much greater range of scales and interrelatedness allowing them not just to identify glyphs in parallel isolation but indeed to read letters and their interrelation and literal intertwinement with other letters and from there words actions practices humans etc thus i am rather confident the first true agi will become so by becoming fluent in reading unicode as a recursive alphabet as you might imagine i have much much more to say about this but i ve already gone on too long if you have a free moment to respond with any thoughts or suggestions for next steps i would very much appreciate it']

re grading in a consistent manner some years ago mit had a project for training ai they wanted to get key words to describe images if there s a barn and a cat in the picture they wanted the key words barn and cat but some people differ on key words some people would say farm or shed so their plan was to generate key words by gamifying the human keyword generation they would show the same picture to two people and if both people said the same keyword they would both get points if not they would both get a new picture makes perfect sense but the keywords they got were almost always just a color after playing the game people independently hit on the idea of just describing the most prevalent color in the image nothing about the actual content at all since this was easily assessed by anyone and if other people were doing it the best way for you to match their key words was to do it as well even for meaningless internet points and knowing full well it worked against the purpose of the project the natural attraction was to use as simple a criteria as possible people independently hit on the idea of merely using a clear and universal but utterly meaningless criteria replace it now with test grading where your goal is to match the score of someone else who may or may not be grading the test you could follow subjective criteria closely but once a few people switch to using simple and objective criteria your best strategy is probably to try to match those as well and the points are no longer meaningless you now get money for it but you say someone else could check the quality of your work of course it is done by private companies who don t want to spend a lot of money looking for problems with their own work they follow their two grader procedure they accurately report any work done outside of class they easily fall into doing this even when explicitly told not to it s just so normal and asking them to work alone is very strange difference is pressure for academic success particularly extrinsic success i e grades over deep learning this can be so ingrained that they feel they must succeed even when they are no longer answerable to anyone but themselves for their grades after growing up that way it s so ingrained in them hence cultural difference is attitude towards cheating no culture really approves of cheating afaik but in n america and europe it is generally considered to be shameful to cheat whether you get caught or not but in some cultures it isn t as shameful unless you get caught in other words there s a different attitude about whether the cheating itself or the getting caught brings more shame amp interact in the attitude towards cheating to get a better grade vs honestly getting a bad grade everyone would agree an honest good grade is best but some cultures would consider it acceptable or even preferable to get a good grade dishonestly than to get a bad grade provided you aren t caught even when getting caught he attitude may be that the mistake wasn t cheating but getting caught considering the alternative may be a bad grade difference is the expectation of exceptions n america and europe generally have the attitude that rules are rules and exceptions generally should not be made in some cultures however it is much more normal that those with the power to do so have both the ability and willingness to make exceptions when they choose to in other words we may say we can t because of the rules even self imposed ones like our own syllabus and really believe we can t but they expect we can and will make exceptions if we want to', 'looks like there are a few questions about how this all works hopefully this will clarify like many i create a bank of questions for an exam the questions are then randomized into three versions in my question bank there are three questions that have very obvious answers within each version these three questions with obvious answers use the same question but the correct answer is different for each version unless you are looking carefully it is really hard to figure out which questions were treated as such because there are three questions you can determine which version each student took with a reasonably high degree of certainty an important key of the design is that the question answers are randomized but the order of questions is identical for all students all students take the exam with the understanding that there is only one version even though there are really three versions in scoring the exams are scored two ways first every student s exam is scored against all three versions the highest score determines the version next the three salted questions are scored to independently determine which version the student took if a student did not cheat the three questions that were salted in should match the version determined independently by scoring against all versions usually less than of students will be in the situation where the salted questions do not match their version determined by their exam score of the time this is due to a student that was either guessing or at the bottom of the class this is easy to tease out using the per question determined correct response rate and i cross check this against the physical exam they returned with their score sheet almost always it is a student that is scoring near which is about the rate of just randomly picking questions where this all breaks down however if a student cheated the salted questions will say they took one version while their exam score will match a different version since most students that cheat seem to at least try the easy questions so they don t get the exact score as who they cheated from if i get a discrepancy i then look to see if there is funny business if they cheated it almost always matches up well with someone in their peer group or lab group since most cheaters are not that sophisticated these seems to do the job for those that wholesale cheat it is really easy to catch as they always cheat from a friend they have groups in their lab section so if i detect and unusually high grade and it is nearly identical to those in their peer groups then we have a chat i have had students that have cheated using hand signals so they are able to communicate surprising effectively i more frequently find that someone is reading off of another student s exam when they don t know the answer s i can also match their per question performance against the class and determine if they had an unusually similar score using the other students this analysis is a bit more tricky but i do machine learning and data analysis as part of my research program so this isn t hard to do for me you can detect cheating by analyzing the student s per item performance compared to the class and determine if they did abnormally well on a certain class of questions i e if you have a student performing on questions that most students answered correctly at gt but the cheating student scored on questions students averaged lt correct then you have something funny going on']
